role: Former Iranian cultural attaché in Buenos Aires; MOIS/Quds Force asset
bio: Mohsen Rabbani served as Iran's cultural attaché in Buenos Aires from 1994 and operated out of the [Islamic Cultural Institute of Buenos Aires](/orgs/islamic-cultural-institute-buenos-aires) (At-Tauhid Mosque). Argentine prosecutors identify him as the on-the-ground coordinator of the [AMIA bombing](/events/amia-bombing-1994) of July 18, 1994, which killed 85 people at the Jewish community centre in Buenos Aires. He is assessed by investigators to be both a [MOIS](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence) asset and affiliated with the [IRGC-Quds Force](/orgs/irgc-quds-force). He is subject to an Interpol red notice and Argentine arrest warrant. After leaving Argentina, Rabbani remained active in Latin America as a recruiter of Shia converts and builder of Hezbollah-linked networks across the region.
editorial_notes: Argentine Interpol red notice and arrest warrant active. Still operating in Latin America per recent intelligence assessments.
